name: Toggle
description: A two-state button that can be on or off. Communicates pressed state to assistive technology via aria-pressed. Built on Base UI Toggle with StyleX styling that mirrors Button.

Root.description: The toggle button. Renders a <button> with aria-pressed semantics. Accepts an icon, text label, or both as children.

variant.description: Visual style of the off-state. Outline has a border, ghost has no border. The on (pressed) state always uses the filled primary appearance, matching Button's solid variant.

size.description: Size of the toggle, mirroring Button. Affects height, padding, and font size.

pressed.description: Whether the toggle is currently pressed. Controlled counterpart of defaultPressed.

defaultPressed.description: Whether the toggle is initially pressed. Uncontrolled counterpart of pressed.

onPressedChange.description: Callback fired when the pressed state changes via click, space, or enter.

value.description: A unique string that identifies the toggle when used inside a ToggleGroup. ToggleGroup uses this to track which child is pressed.

disabled.description: Whether the toggle should ignore user interaction.

sx.description: StyleX styles for consumer overrides. Applied last.

dataAttributes.data-pressed: Present when the toggle is on.
dataAttributes.data-unpressed: Present when the toggle is off.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the toggle is disabled.

intents.reasoning: Toggle is a button that announces its on/off state via aria-pressed. Use when a single button should flip between two states (e.g., bold formatting in a text editor).
Icon-only toggle is the standard pattern for editor formatting controls and stateful icon buttons. Always provide aria-label since there is no visible text.
A pressed Toggle reads as an active filter chip and announces its state to assistive tech. Use ghost variant inside a Toolbar or filter bar to avoid stacking borders.

avoidWhen.scenario: Settings change that takes effect immediately (e.g., dark mode)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Switch is the conventional control for an immediate on/off setting and is more easily recognized as a toggle by users.
Use ToggleGroup, RadioGroup, or Tabs — Toggle alone has no concept of mutual exclusion.
Toggle represents persistent state. Use Button for actions that fire and don't stay on.

avoidWhen.scenario: A boolean that submits with a form
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Checkbox — it integrates with form submission and is the conventional control for boolean form fields.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Uncontrolled toggle with a text label

examples.name: controlled
examples.description: Controlled toggle driven by React state
examples.code:
const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);

<Toggle.Root pressed={pressed} onPressedChange={setPressed}>
  Italic
</Toggle.Root>

examples.name: ghost
examples.description: Ghost variant for use inside toolbars and Toggle Groups

examples.name: disabled
examples.description: Disabled toggle in the pressed state
